steps.title: Find the time anchor
steps.content: The phrase "By the end of the 1990s" fixes the action at a completed point in the past. Any verb here must express an action that was finished before that specific past moment.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong tenses and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice:
  • Choice A — has doubled: present perfect — cannot be paired with a specific past time marker like "by the end of the 1990s." ✗
  • Choice B — had doubled: past perfect — correctly shows the doubling was completed before a specific past point. ✓
  • Choice C — doubles: simple present — contradicts the past timeframe entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — will double: future tense — the opposite of a completed past action. ✗
B is correct.
